constraints.text: Screenshots are temporary QA artifacts and must never be committed.
Raw sessions remain local-only and are never committed as frontend QA evidence.

decisions.text: Frontend dashboard work uses a shared frontend visual QA skill and screenshot-based review rather than code-only inspection.
Clickable-looking UI elements must have an explicit interaction contract for graph navigation, logs navigation, nodes, edges, and inspector actions.

requirements.text: Dashboard changes that affect rendering should be verified with Playwright screenshots for graph and logs pages.
Temporary screenshots must be inspected locally and deleted before commit.

summary: The frontend visual QA workflow became a global contract for the dashboard: use the frontend-visual-qa skill, capture Playwright screenshots, inspect them locally, delete them afterward, and enforce a clickable interaction contract for graph and logs UI.
